what_manipulated: five-factor personality trait differences (neuroticism, extraversion, openness, agreeableness, conscientiousness)
what_measured: cortical morphometry measures (thickness, surface area, folding) via structural MRI
experiment: Correlate individual differences in personality scores (NEO-FFI) with regional variations in cortical thickness, surface area, and folding across the whole brain. Multivariate GLM tests associations at each vertex, controlling for age, intracranial volume, and sex, with cluster-wise multiple comparison correction.

what_manipulated: primary-grade retention status (retained versus non-retained in K-5)
what_measured: high school completion, college entry, college completion
experiment: Estimate causal effect of early grade retention on secondary and postsecondary educational transitions using propensity score matching and sibling fixed-effects models. Control for pre-retention confounders via matching and regression; test mediation through middle and early high school academic achievements and expectations.

what_manipulated: time horizon condition (unspecified, expanded, or limited) via written scenario
what_measured: choice of work activity: help colleague, career-advancing project, or company-exploration project
experiment: Randomly assign employed participants to one of three time-horizon conditions; measure forced-choice preference for emotionally meaningful (helping) versus future-oriented work activities. Test whether age-related preference for helping is mediated by time horizon constraints.

what_manipulated: number of Facebook friends (online social network size)
what_measured: grey matter density at multiple brain regions via structural MRI
experiment: Correlate Facebook friend count with regional grey matter density using voxel-based morphometry in a primary sample; replicate in an independent sample. Test whether online social network size correlates with real-world network size and whether brain regions are specific to online versus offline networks.

what_manipulated: action mode (alone versus joint action with partner; uni-manual versus bi-manual in solo condition)
what_measured: proportion of together choices; trial time; movement time; accuracy of task completion
experiment: Participants choose repeatedly between acting alone or jointly on a touch-screen task; jointly clearing items incurs coordination costs but yields equal reward per action as solo mode. Measure preference for joint action despite higher instrumental costs. Include solo control (uni- vs. bi-manual) and manipulate difficulty.

what_manipulated: code snippet variant (method chaining present/absent; comment quality: good/bad/none)
what_measured: perceived readability (1-5 scale); reading time; completion task accuracy (cloze test)
experiment: Present five Java code snippets in systematically varied versions (two method-chain levels × three comment levels = six variants per snippet). Measure perceived readability and comprehension via reading time and cloze-test accuracy. Between-subjects blocking on snippet sequence; control for prior programming experience.
